summary: Full backend and frontend testing of VC Deal Flow tool. All 15 backend tests pass. Frontend core flows work - dashboard, filters, detail panel, process email modal, settings navigation all functional. Key issues: sample deals disappear once user processes any email (design bug), disconnect-gmail-btn testid mismatch, filter score testid mismatch.

minor.issue: When user has any user_deal, sample deals (VaultAI, GreenLoop, LP Partners, CloudBase) are hidden - `combined = user_deals + (sample_deals if not user_deals else [])`. Running backend tests created a TestCorp deal for test user, causing the dashboard to show only 1 deal instead of 4 sample deals.

ui_bugs.issue: data-testid='disconnect-gmail-btn' does NOT exist - since test user has no Gmail connected, button shows 'Connect Gmail' with testid='connect-gmail-settings-btn'. Disconnect button is only visible when gmail_connected=true.
back-btn data-testid is actually 'back-to-dashboard' not 'back-btn'. Minor testid naming inconsistency.

integration_issues.issue: Modal backdrop (bg-black/70 backdrop-blur-sm div) intercepts pointer events preventing playwright automated click on Cancel button. Works fine for real users. Force-click on modal close X button may also be affected.

issues: data-testid for 'Score >= 7' filter is 'filter-score---7' (unicode ≥ replaced with hyphens) - review_request referenced it as filter-score->=-7 which is wrong. Actual testid: filter-score---7

action_items: BUG: Sample deals (VaultAI etc) disappear when user has any deal - should always show sample deals OR show sample deals alongside user deals for new users. Current logic hides samples entirely once user has 1 deal.

MINOR: The 'Disconnect Gmail' button (data-testid=disconnect-gmail-btn) in settings only appears when gmail_connected=true. Consider adding testid documentation note, or always render button conditionally.
MINOR: Filter Score testid is 'filter-score---7' (actual unicode), review_request listed wrong testid. Not a code bug.
MINOR: Settings back button testid is 'back-to-dashboard', review_request listed 'back-btn'.
critical_code_review_comments: Sample deals logic bug (server.py line 736): `combined = user_deals + (sample_deals if not user_deals else [])` - This means once a user processes even 1 email manually, ALL 4 sample deals vanish from their dashboard and stats. The sample deals become invisible. This is likely unintended behavior for the demo UX.
server.py PATCH /deals/{id} does NOT check ownership - any authenticated user can update any deal by ID. Security concern for multi-user setup.

success_rate.frontend: 85% - all core flows work, minor testid mismatches and sample deal visibility issue

seed_data_creation: Backend test created TEST_John Founder / TestCorp deal for test user c054bacc-7256-41ab-b613-1379fb8ee883 via POST /api/deals/process
context_for_next_testing_agent: Test user (c054bacc-...) now has 1 user deal (TestCorp) in Supabase, so sample deals are hidden from their dashboard. To test sample deals visibility, either delete the TestCorp deal from Supabase or use a fresh user. Backend is 100% healthy. Claude AI (claude-sonnet-4-5) working. Supabase connected.

rca_of_issues.sample_deals_disappear: server.py line 736: `combined = user_deals + (sample_deals if not user_deals else [])`. When user_deals is non-empty list, sample_deals is excluded entirely. The backend test (TestProcessEmail) created a deal for the test user, making user_deals non-empty. Fix: always include sample deals, or only hide them for users with >N real deals.
rca_of_issues.modal_backdrop_block: ProcessEmailModal.jsx has a `fixed inset-0 z-50` parent with a `absolute inset-0 bg-black/70 backdrop-blur-sm` child. The backdrop div covers the entire modal container including the Cancel button area. Playwright's standard click fails because the overlay intercepts events. Works in real browser since clicks pass through correctly on actual modal buttons.
